projects.title: DOM Tree Explorer
projects.description: Write a function that logs the entire DOM tree structure starting from the document root, showing each element's tag name and text content.
projects.exampleText: Open any webpage and run the script in the console.
projects.exampleOutput: A nested tree of nodes printed to the console.
projects.learningOutcome: Understand the DOM hierarchy, recursion, node traversal.
projects.logicExplanation: The DOM (Document Object Model) represents the structure of an HTML document as a tree of nodes. Each element, attribute, and text becomes a node. Understanding this tree is key to dynamic web pages.
projects.codeExplanation: This script logs the DOM tree structure. It recursively walks through the document, printing the tag name or text content of each node, showing the hierarchy.

projects.title: Select Elements Practice
projects.description: Create an HTML page with various elements (id, class, attributes). Write JavaScript to select and log these elements using getElementById, querySelector, and querySelectorAll.
projects.exampleText:
HTML:
Hello

Text
projects.exampleOutput: Console shows selected elements.
projects.learningOutcome: Using different selection methods, understanding NodeList vs HTMLCollection.
projects.logicExplanation: To manipulate the DOM, you first need to select elements. `getElementById` selects by id, `querySelector` uses CSS selectors and returns the first match, while `querySelectorAll` returns a list.
projects.codeExplanation: The code demonstrates how to select elements using various methods, then logs the selected elements to the console. It also shows how to get a collection and loop through it.

projects.title: Dynamic Content Changer
projects.description: Write a script that changes the text, HTML, and styles of selected elements when a button is clicked.
projects.exampleText: Button click triggers changes.
projects.exampleOutput: The content and style of elements update dynamically.
projects.learningOutcome: Manipulating innerHTML, textContent, and CSS properties via JavaScript.
projects.logicExplanation: Once an element is selected, you can change its content and style. Use `textContent` or `innerHTML` for text, and `style` property for CSS. This allows real-time updates without reloading the page.
projects.codeExplanation: This script picks elements and modifies their text, HTML, and CSS. It includes examples of changing colors, adding new content, and toggling visibility.

projects.title: Interactive To-Do List
projects.description: Build a to-do list where users can add new tasks, mark them as completed, and delete them. Use DOM creation and removal methods.
projects.exampleText: Input field + Add button; each task has complete and delete buttons.
projects.exampleOutput: Tasks appear and can be managed interactively.
projects.learningOutcome: Creating elements dynamically, event delegation, removing elements.
projects.logicExplanation: Dynamic creation and removal of elements is essential for interactive UIs. You can use `createElement`, `appendChild`, `removeChild`, and newer methods like `append` and `remove`.
projects.codeExplanation: A simple to-do list is implemented. The user can add tasks (creating elements), mark them complete, and delete them (removing elements). This demonstrates event handling and dynamic DOM updates.

projects.title: Attribute & Class Manipulator
projects.description: Create a set of buttons that demonstrate how to get/set attributes, toggle classes, and read/write data attributes on an element.
projects.exampleText: A div with attributes and classes; buttons to modify them.
projects.exampleOutput: The div's appearance and attributes change on button clicks.
projects.learningOutcome: Using getAttribute, setAttribute, classList, dataset.
projects.logicExplanation: Attributes, classes, and data attributes let you store extra information and apply styling. Use `getAttribute`, `setAttribute`, `classList` methods, and `dataset` to manipulate them.
projects.codeExplanation: The script shows how to get and set attributes, add/remove/toggle CSS classes, and read/write custom data attributes. Buttons and inputs demonstrate each operation.

projects.title: Event Handling Demo
projects.description: Create a page with several elements (button, div, input) and attach event handlers for click, mouseover, and keypress events. Show messages in a console or on screen.
projects.exampleText: Click the button, hover over a div, type in input.
projects.exampleOutput: Console logs or on-screen messages appear indicating which event fired.
projects.learningOutcome: Adding event listeners, event object, handling different event types.
projects.logicExplanation: Events allow JavaScript to react to user actions. Common events include click, mouseover, keydown, etc. Event listeners can be attached to elements to trigger functions.
projects.codeExplanation: This code demonstrates attaching event listeners to various elements. It shows click, mouseenter/mouseleave, and keypress events, logging messages and updating the UI accordingly.

projects.title: Form Input Tracker
projects.description: Create a form with an input field and a paragraph. As the user types, display the current input value in the paragraph (live preview).
projects.exampleText: Type something in the input field.
projects.exampleOutput: The paragraph updates in real time with the same text.
projects.learningOutcome: Using the input event, updating DOM on every keystroke.
projects.logicExplanation: Real‑time form tracking uses input events to capture user keystrokes and display them elsewhere. This is useful for previews or live validation.
projects.codeExplanation: The script listens to the `input` event on a text field and displays the current value in another element, updating on every keystroke.

projects.title: Simple Image Slider
projects.description: Build an image slider with previous/next buttons. Display an image, its caption, and allow navigation through a set of images.
projects.exampleText: Click 'Next' or 'Previous' to cycle through images.
projects.exampleOutput: The main image and caption change accordingly.
projects.learningOutcome: Array indexing, updating image src and text, event handling.
projects.logicExplanation: An image slider cycles through a set of images using next/previous buttons. It can also support automatic sliding. This uses array indexing and updating the `src` attribute of an image element.
projects.codeExplanation: The code maintains an array of image URLs. On button clicks, it increments/decrements an index and updates the main image's `src`. It also updates the caption.

projects.title: Modal Dialog
projects.description: Create a modal dialog that appears when a button is clicked, and can be closed via a close button or clicking outside the modal.
projects.exampleText: Click 'Open Modal', then close it.
projects.exampleOutput: A centered modal appears/disappears.
projects.learningOutcome: CSS display toggling, event propagation, overlay handling.
projects.logicExplanation: A modal is a dialog box that appears on top of the page, disabling background interaction. It is typically hidden until triggered, and can be closed by a button or clicking outside.
projects.codeExplanation: The script shows/hides a modal overlay by toggling CSS classes. It includes an open button, a close button, and an optional click outside the modal content to close.

projects.title: Accordion Component
projects.description: Build an accordion with at least three sections. Each section has a header; clicking a header expands its content and collapses others.
projects.exampleText: Click on section headers.
projects.exampleOutput: Only one content panel is visible at a time.
projects.learningOutcome: Element selection, sibling traversal, toggling classes.
projects.logicExplanation: An accordion consists of collapsible sections. Clicking a header expands or collapses its associated content. This improves space usage and user experience.
projects.codeExplanation: The code selects all accordion headers. On header click, it toggles the visibility of the corresponding content and optionally closes other open sections for a typical accordion behaviour.

projects.title: Responsive Hamburger Menu
projects.description: Create a responsive navigation bar that collapses into a hamburger icon on small screens. Clicking the icon toggles the menu visibility.
projects.exampleText: Resize browser window and click the hamburger icon.
projects.exampleOutput: Menu expands/collapses when icon is clicked.
projects.learningOutcome: CSS media queries, class toggling, responsive design.
projects.logicExplanation: A hamburger menu is a common pattern for mobile navigation. Clicking the icon toggles the visibility of a navigation menu, often using CSS transforms or max-height transitions.
projects.codeExplanation: The script adds a click listener to the hamburger icon. It toggles a class on the navigation menu that controls its display or height, making it responsive.

projects.title: Sortable List
projects.description: Implement a drag-and-drop reorderable list. Allow users to rearrange list items by dragging and dropping them.
projects.exampleText: Drag items in the list.
projects.exampleOutput: Items can be moved to different positions.
projects.learningOutcome: HTML5 Drag and Drop API, event handling, DOM manipulation.
projects.logicExplanation: Sortable lists allow users to reorder items by dragging and dropping. This can be implemented using the HTML5 Drag and Drop API or with mouse events.
projects.codeExplanation: This code uses the HTML5 Drag and Drop API. Each list item has `draggable=true`. Events like `dragstart`, `dragover`, and `drop` are handled to rearrange items in the list.

projects.title: Local Storage Notes App
projects.description: Build a simple notes app where users can add, edit, and delete notes. All notes should be saved to localStorage so they persist after page reload.
projects.exampleText: Add a note, refresh the page.
projects.exampleOutput: The note still appears after refresh.
projects.learningOutcome: localStorage, JSON parsing/stringifying, CRUD operations.
projects.logicExplanation: Local storage allows saving data in the browser persistently. A notes app can store notes as strings, retrieve them on page load, and update when notes are added or removed.
projects.codeExplanation: The script loads notes from localStorage on page load. Users can add, edit, and delete notes; each change saves the updated array back to localStorage. The notes are dynamically rendered as list items.

projects.title: Infinite Scroll
projects.description: Create a page that loads more items (e.g., cards) as the user scrolls near the bottom. Use fake data or fetch from a simulated API.
projects.exampleText: Scroll to the bottom of the page.
projects.exampleOutput: New items are appended dynamically.
projects.learningOutcome: Scroll event, debouncing, dynamic content loading.
projects.logicExplanation: Infinite scroll loads more content as the user scrolls near the bottom. It detects scroll position and fetches additional items (here simulated with fake data).
projects.codeExplanation: The code listens to the window's scroll event. When scroll position is near the bottom, it calls a function to append new items to the container, simulating an API call.

projects.title: Dynamic Table Generator
projects.description: Given an array of objects (e.g., students with name, age, grade), generate an HTML table dynamically and display it on the page.
projects.exampleText: Use the provided data (or input).
projects.exampleOutput: A table with headers and rows appears.
projects.learningOutcome: Creating tables dynamically, innerHTML vs createElement.
projects.logicExplanation: Dynamic table generation creates HTML tables from JavaScript data. It is useful for displaying structured data like CSV, JSON, or database results.
projects.codeExplanation: This script builds a table element from an array of objects. It creates the `<table>`, `<thead>`, and `<tbody>`, populates rows and cells, and appends it to the DOM.

projects.title: Form Validation
projects.description: Create a registration form with fields: name, email, password, confirm password. Validate on submit and show error messages. Prevent submission if invalid.
projects.exampleText: Fill and submit the form.
projects.exampleOutput: Error messages appear for invalid fields; if valid, an alert or success message is shown.
projects.learningOutcome: Form validation, regex, event.preventDefault, error display.
projects.logicExplanation: Form validation checks user input before submission. It can validate required fields, email format, password strength, and provide immediate feedback.
projects.codeExplanation: The code adds event listeners to form fields and the submit button. It validates each field (e.g., email regex, password length) and displays error messages, preventing submission if invalid.

projects.title: Tabbed Interface
projects.description: Create a tab component with at least three tabs. Clicking a tab shows its content and hides the others.
projects.exampleText: Click different tab buttons.
projects.exampleOutput: Only the content of the selected tab is visible.
projects.learningOutcome: CSS class switching, managing multiple content panels.
projects.logicExplanation: Tabs allow switching between different content panels without reloading the page. Each tab button hides its corresponding content and shows the selected one.
projects.codeExplanation: The script attaches click handlers to tab buttons. It removes an 'active' class from all content panels and adds it to the one associated with the clicked tab.

projects.title: Search Filter
projects.description: Create a search box that filters a list of items as the user types. Only items containing the search term should remain visible.
projects.exampleText: Type a word in the search box.
projects.exampleOutput: The list updates in real time, showing only matching items.
projects.learningOutcome: Input event, filtering arrays, toggling display.
projects.logicExplanation: A search filter dynamically shows/hides list items based on user input. It can be used for search boxes, filtering product lists, etc.
projects.codeExplanation: The code listens to the input event on a search box. For each list item, it compares the item's text with the search term (case‑insensitive) and toggles the `display` style.

projects.title: Countdown Timer
projects.description: Create a countdown timer where the user can set minutes/seconds, start, pause, and reset. Display the remaining time and play an alert when finished.
projects.exampleText: Set 1 minute, start the timer.
projects.exampleOutput: Timer counts down to zero, then alerts.
projects.learningOutcome: setInterval/clearInterval, state management, user input handling.
projects.logicExplanation: A countdown timer uses `setInterval` to decrement time and update the display. It should stop when reaching zero and optionally trigger a callback.
projects.codeExplanation: The script gets the target time (or seconds from input) and starts an interval that updates the display every second. When time runs out, it clears the interval and shows a message.

projects.title: Custom Tooltip
projects.description: Create a custom tooltip that appears when hovering over elements with a data-tooltip attribute. The tooltip should follow the mouse or be positioned near the element.
projects.exampleText: Hover over any element with a data-tooltip attribute.
projects.exampleOutput: A tooltip appears with the attribute's text.
projects.learningOutcome: Mouse events, dynamic element creation and removal, positioning.
projects.logicExplanation: Tooltips provide additional information on hover. A custom tooltip can be created by showing/hiding a div near the cursor or target element using mouse events.
projects.codeExplanation: The code selects elements with a `data-tooltip` attribute. On mouseenter, it creates or shows a tooltip element with the attribute's text, positions it, and on mouseleave hides/destroys it.
